lsp.justification: The answer correctly identifies the shared construction site as checkType() in src/object.c with addReplyErrorObject(c, shared.wrongtypeerr), and traces both SET-specific paths: SET ... GET through setGenericCommand -> getGenericCommand -> t_string.c:467, and the IFEQ/IFNE/IFDEQ/IFDNE branch at setGenericCommand around t_string.c:117-121. It also explicitly notes that plain SET overwrites without WRONGTYPE and distinguishes the shared GET/checkType helper from other commands' paths.
